language.title: Choose your language
language.subtitle: Which language should the interface use? You can change this later in Settings.

welcome.title: Welcome to Ember
welcome.description: Meeting transcription and summaries — entirely on your device. Let's set it up in a minute.

local.title: Local and private
local.caption: Audio and text never leave your computer

offline.title: Works offline
offline.caption: Models run directly on your device

export.title: Export to Markdown
export.caption: Summaries are ready for Obsidian instantly

setup.title: Getting ready
setup.description: To work, Ember needs to download AI models for speech recognition and note-taking.

transcription.title: Speech recognition engine
transcription.caption: Whisper transcribes audio to text — locally

summarization.title: AI for notes
summarization.caption: Turns the transcript into a structured summary
summarization.tooltip: You can also choose external AI providers (OpenAI, Claude, Ollama) in settings.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Ember needs access to the microphone and system audio to record meetings

microphone.title: Microphone
microphone.description: Required to record your voice during meetings
microphone.settingsAlert: Allow microphone access in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone

systemAudio.title: System audio
systemAudio.description: Click “Allow” to grant audio capture access
systemAudio.settingsAlert: Allow audio capture in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Audio Capture

permissions.noAccessNote: Recording won't work without these permissions. You can grant them later in settings.

row.denied: Access denied — grant it in System Settings

download.title: Downloading models
download.description: Choose a Whisper model for speech recognition and a local AI model for notes, then wait for the download.
download.summaryHint: Choose and download any model. Qwen2.5 7B is noticeably smarter than Gemma and better at Russian — recommended if you have enough memory (~6 GB).
